name: Texas Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup unsweetened dark chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and sugar.
2. In a heavy saucepan, melt butter and shortening over medium heat.
3. Add coffee (or water) and cocoa to the melted butter mixture. Stir until combined.
4. Heat the mixture to boiling, stirring occasionally.
5. Pour the hot butter mixture over the flour and sugar in the bowl.
6. Add eggs, baking soda, and vanilla. Mix well with a wooden spoon or electric mixer on high speed.
7. Grease a 17 1/2 x 11 inch jelly roll pan with butter.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake at 400°F for 20 minutes or until set in the center.
9. While the brownies are baking, prepare the frosting:
- In another saucepan, melt the butter and add cocoa and milk.
- Heat to boiling, stirring constantly.
- Gradually mix in powdered sugar and vanilla until smooth.
10. Pour the warm frosting over the brownies as soon as they come out of the oven.
11. Allow the brownies to cool completely before cutting into bars.
